preview_text:
The model rates Argentina as a heavy favourite at 76%, leaving Austria just 4% to claim victory. This is a stark asymmetry: the underdog's win probability sits roughly one-fifth of the favourite's. Argentina's baseline advantage appears substantial across the statistical framework.

The win-draw-loss spread reflects a lopsided contest. A draw at 20% is plausible but not central to the forecast; the model sees Argentina's win as the dominant outcome. The gap between Argentina's 76% and Austria's 4% is wide enough to suggest limited competitive balance in the model's view, though the 20% draw rate leaves room for a stalemate rather than a runaway result.

The model and sportsbook consensus diverge meaningfully. Bookmakers assign Austria 17% win probability—more than four times the model's 4%—and compress Argentina to 58%. Polymarket sits between them, closer to the sportsbook line. The dashboard makes no claim about which assessment is correct; the divergence is notable and worth monitoring as new information emerges.
